title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Oyster Bay, NY | $900–$2,500 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Oyster Bay, Long Island. Humane, safe bat control for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Oyster Bay's beautiful historic homes and waterfront properties can unfortunately attract bats seeking shelter in attics, chimneys, and eaves. Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Oyster Bay, ensuring your property is safely and humanely freed from bat infestations. Our experienced technicians use proven methods to remove bats and prevent their return, protecting both your family and these important wildlife species.
whyUs: With over a decade of experience serving Long Island communities, Best Pest Control NYC specializes in humane bat removal that complies with New York wildlife protection laws. Our Oyster Bay team provides thorough inspections, safe exclusion techniques, cleanup of contaminated areas, and guaranteed prevention solutions tailored to your property's unique architecture.
localContext: Oyster Bay's mix of historic estates, traditional colonials near Theodore Roosevelt's Sagamore Hill, and waterfront homes along the harbor create numerous entry points where bats commonly roost during warmer months. The area's mature trees and proximity to wooded preserves make bat activity particularly common in residential neighborhoods from Centre Island to Cove Neck and downtown Oyster Bay.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Oyster Bay?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Oyster Bay typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and extent of exclusion work needed. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: When is bat removal season in Oyster Bay?
faqs.answer: The best time for bat exclusion in Oyster Bay is during spring (April-May) or fall (August-October). New York law prohibits bat exclusion from June 1 to July 31 during maternity season when flightless young bats are present. Our team schedules services accordingly to ensure humane and legal removal.

faqs.question: Are bats common in Oyster Bay homes?
faqs.answer: Yes, bats are quite common in Oyster Bay due to the area's abundant woodland habitat, proximity to water, and older homes with accessible entry points. Little brown bats and big brown bats frequently roost in attics, soffits, and wall voids. If you hear scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, you may have bats.

faqs.question: Is bat guano dangerous to my family?
faqs.answer: Yes, bat droppings (guano) can pose serious health risks as they may contain histoplasmosis spores, which can cause respiratory illness when airborne particles are inhaled. Our team safely removes contaminated insulation, sanitizes affected areas, and restores your attic to safe conditions as part of our comprehensive bat removal service.
